question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with startTime=0 (satisfying @Min(0)) to subscribe to all historical topic messages including fee schedule update messages from the genesis of the ledger, bypassing the intended 'current time' default, causing unauthorized access to historical transaction fee data outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage message, TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that filterMessage() only checks topicId equality and consensusTimestamp >= startTime with no fee-content validation, allowing them to receive all messages on a fee-schedule topic (e.g., Hedera system topic 0.0.111) and observe every fee parameter change in real time, enabling fee manipulation awareness outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage topicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user publish a topic message with a crafted consensusTimestamp value near Long.MAX_VALUE such that the division consensusTimestamp / 1000000 overflows or produces an astronomically large value, causing System.currentTimeMillis() - (consensusTimestamp / 1000000) to produce a negative latency that is silently discarded (latency > 0 check), poisoning the consensus latency metric and masking real fee-related message delivery delays?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=0 in TopicMessageFilter (satisfying @Min(0)), causing hasLimit() to return false and the polling listener to use Integer.MAX_VALUE as the page size, resulting in unbounded retrieval of fee-related topic messages that overwhelms the server and causes denial of service affecting fee message delivery for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the default branch of the switch statement in getTopicListener() by causing an unexpected ListenerType value, resulting in an UnsupportedOperationException that leaks the internal listener type configuration string to the client, revealing infrastructure details that could be used to craft targeted fee-manipulation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage message, TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of an upper-bound check on consensusTimestamp in filterMessage() (only >= startTime is checked, no <= endTime enforcement at this layer) to receive fee-related messages beyond an intended subscription window, allowing them to observe fee changes that should have been outside their authorized time range?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of concurrent gRPC subscriptions each with a different TopicMessageFilter targeting fee-schedule topics, exhausting the bounded elastic scheduler thread pool used by PollingTopicListener, causing legitimate fee update notifications to be delayed or dropped outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage topicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a topic message with consensusTimestamp=0, causing the division 0/1000000=0 and latency = System.currentTimeMillis() - 0 = a very large positive number, which gets recorded into the consensusLatencyTimer metric, skewing the consensus latency histogram and causing operators to misinterpret fee message delivery performance outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to Long.MAX_VALUE in TopicMessageFilter (no upper bound validation in isValidEndTime() beyond endTime > startTime), causing the subscription to remain open indefinitely and consume server resources while continuously receiving all future fee-related topic messages, enabling persistent unauthorized fee surveillance?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage message, TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that filterMessage() performs no authorization check on whether the requesting user is permitted to subscribe to a specific topicId (e.g., a fee-schedule system topic), allowing any user to subscribe to fee-parameter topics and receive all fee modification events outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with startTime set to the exact nanosecond timestamp of a known fee schedule update transaction, causing the >= comparison in filterMessage() to include that specific fee-change message, allowing targeted retrieval of fee modification events that reveal the exact parameters changed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user, by flooding the system with subscriptions when the REDIS listener is active, cause Redis connection exhaustion that forces a fallback or error state, disrupting fee-related message delivery for all subscribers and causing fee update messages to be missed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.empty() short-circuit when listenerProperties.isEnabled() is false to infer the enabled/disabled state of the listener by observing whether they receive an immediate empty stream vs. actual messages, leaking infrastructure configuration that could be used to time fee-manipulation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage message, TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the long comparison message.getConsensusTimestamp() >= filter.getStartTime() where both are signed longs, by setting startTime to a negative value (if validation is bypassed upstream), causing all messages including fee-related ones with any positive timestamp to pass the filter, resulting in unauthorized access to all fee modification history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a fee-schedule topic using the SHARED_POLL listener type (SharedPollingTopicListener), which shares a single global poll across all subscribers, and by doing so cause their subscription to receive messages intended for other subscribers' fee-topic streams, leading to cross-subscriber fee data leakage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage topicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a message with a consensusTimestamp exactly equal to System.currentTimeMillis() * 1000000 such that the computed latency is exactly 0, causing the latency > 0 guard to suppress metric recording, creating a blind spot in the consensus latency monitoring for fee-related messages and masking fee update delivery anomalies?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with a very small startTime (e.g., 1 nanosecond epoch) to the fee-schedule topic, causing the PollingTopicListener to execute a database query spanning the entire history of fee changes, resulting in a full-table scan that degrades database performance and disrupts fee message delivery for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage message, TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of message content validation in filterMessage() to receive fee-related topic messages whose payload contains raw fee schedule protobuf data, decode the message bytes client-side, and reconstruct the full fee parameter set outside of any access control, enabling fee manipulation awareness?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive Flux pipeline's doOnNext(this::recordMetric) to cause a metric recording side effect for every fee-related message they receive, incrementing the consensusLatencyTimer counter and artificially inflating metric counts that operators use to make fee-related infrastructure decisions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user, by subscribing to fee-schedule topics under the POLL listener type, cause the PollingTopicListener to repeatedly query the database at the configured interval for their specific filter, creating a per-user polling loop that multiplies database load proportionally to the number of attacker-controlled subscriptions, degrading fee message delivery performance?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user set subscriberId to a predictable or empty string (the @Builder.Default generates a random 8-char string but there is no server-side uniqueness enforcement), allowing them to impersonate another subscriber's session ID and potentially interfere with fee-related message delivery tracking for that subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage message, TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that filterMessage() uses message.getTopicId().equals(filter.getTopicId()) with EntityId equality semantics, and if EntityId.equals() has a flaw or considers shard/realm/num separately, craft a topicId that matches a fee-schedule topic through an alternative representation, bypassing intended topic isolation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user rapidly open and close gRPC subscriptions to fee-schedule topics, causing repeated subscribe/unsubscribe cycles in the RedisTopicListener's topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap (computeIfAbsent / remove race), leading to a race condition where fee messages are dropped between unsubscribe and re-subscribe, causing fee update delivery gaps?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage topicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user flood the system with fee-related topic messages each having a unique consensusTimestamp, causing the consensusLatencyTimer (a Micrometer Timer) to accumulate an unbounded number of recordings, exhausting JVM heap memory through metric histogram bucket overflow and causing an out-of-memory condition that disrupts fee message processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on the listen() entry point to open an arbitrarily large number of concurrent Flux subscriptions to fee-schedule topics, each consuming a thread from the bounded elastic scheduler, exhausting the thread pool and causing fee update messages to queue indefinitely outside design parameters?
